The check for ubuntu bionic 18.04 specifically is removed.
The check if resolved is enabled is made with check_service_active()
An additional check that the dnsstublistener is enabled is made.

`shellcheck -x` for following files.

Signed-off-by: Adam Warner <adamw@rner.email>

# Conflicts:
#	advanced/Scripts/update.sh
ShellCheck
Make Chronometer usable on smaller screens (#1518)
more shellcheck fixes

Integrate DL's update.sh fixes.

`shellcheck -x` for following files.

Do not test for included files SC1091

Signed-off-by: Dan Schaper <dan.schaper@pi-hole.net>

Colourise Core Output Text (#1471)

* Define colours within COL_TABLE
* Do not output colours for non-terminal instances
* Removed ":::"
* Fixed indenting & spacing
* Made output consistent throughout project
* Reworded text to fit on standard 80 char wide Terminal screen
* Made 'sudo raspi-config' warning (insufficient disk space) only show on RPi
* Make "Installation/Update Complete" the final msg
* Remove redundant messages
* Simplify update available message
* Confirm user would like to begin uninstall
* If "git pull" string says "Already up-to-date.", place [i] before it
* Colour Temp/Interface output
* Made `pihole disable 5z` invalid
* Added error fallback if invalid argument (not s/m) is detected
* Quoted "$2" for consistency
* Updated help text
* L185/286: Replaced echo with redirect
* User agents for adblock.mahakala.is/adaway.org unnecessary 
* Print newline on confirmation of repository reset
* Add output to admin-related dnsmasq restarts
* Return error message for "pihole -q"
* Imply default checkout behaviour with y/N
* Fix uninstall failing to remove pihole user
* Print checkout 'git remote show origin' STDERR on new line
* Replaced checkout "AdminLTE" wording with "Web Admin"
removed_lines: -  git remote show origin | grep 'tracked' | sed 's/tracked//;s/ //g'
added_lines: +  output=$( { git remote show origin | grep 'tracked' | sed 's/tracked//;s/ //g'; } 2>&1 )

pi-hole/pi-hole.message: Introduce new file black.list for blacklist content
removed_lines: -      cat ${piholeDir}/${eventHorizon} | awk -v ipv4addr="$IPV4_ADDRESS" -v ipv6addr="$IPV6_ADDRESS" '{sub(/\r$/,""); print ipv4addr" "$0"\n"ipv6addr" "$0}' >> ${piholeDir}/${accretionDisc}
added_lines: +      cat "${1}" | awk -v ipv4addr="$IPV4_ADDRESS" -v ipv6addr="$IPV6_ADDRESS" '{sub(/\r$/,""); print ipv4addr" "$0"\n"ipv6addr" "$0}' >> "${2}"

Revert "new command to print both the IPv4 and IPv6 address on their own line"

This reverts commit 88e6ce041d112be9ccae654bad3309c7f59a308e.

new command to print both the IPv4 and IPv6 address on their own line

I'm not 100% sure if you can add multiple IP addresses to one domain on
a single line, so I just broke it up into two.  It makes the file
bigger and longer, but I'll have to see how it works

Replace use of grep -w with grep -x.
Prepend "^" to start of latentWhitelist.txt lines.

The -x switch requires a full line match of the regexp, where as -w
will try to find the match somewhere in the line, looking for work
breaks. Combined with turning the whitelist lines into full regexps,
this results in significantly faster parsing.

Having "^" prepended to the lines also keeps false whitelisting from
occuring, such as the following example:

If whitelist.txt contains "google.com" it would whitelist many other
sites that end in "google.com" as long as there is a non-word
character preceeding the google (such as "-", or ".").

Remove leading and trailing whitespace and `.` characters and
duplicate `.` characters as each list is stored.

Should fix #32.

Use `url` variable to store `${sources[$i]}` value to improve readability.

I also wanted to replace the for loop iterating over indices with
something like:

`for url in $sources[@]}`

It made the use of `$i` in the save location more annoying though.

Missed a change todaysAdsEliminated to /\/etc\/pihole\/gravity.list/

resolves #25

Will not count blank lines if they happen to exist.

Parses host only file formats now.  Previously only handled hosts file format.

Specifically, it can now handle the following list:
'http://mirror1.malwaredomains.com/files/justdomains'

uses a variable for hostname instead of raspberrypi

Some people use a hostname other than raspberrypi, so their hostname
did not resolve to 127.0.0.1.  I replaced that hardcoded value with a
variable so that does not happen.

I also added a few comments and minor formatting adjustments,.

preparing files for merge and for testing automated install

I haven't merged anything before, so hopefully, I won't mess it up too
bad!
removed_lines: -echo "  Pi-hole IP: $(ifconfig eth0 | awk '/inet addr/ {print $2}' | cut -d':' -f2)"

pi-hole/pi-hole.message:
Ditching the use of the loopback

Pushing files so they are available when the new article gets posted.

If the Pi's loopback is set in the hosts file, clients using it as a
DNS server will try to connect to their own loopback, which does not
have a Web server.  So the real IP of the Pi is used.  It is
recommended to use a static IP since this will be acting as a server.

Made one small change from some hard coded values to a variable.

add IPv6 support in the hosts file

Still need to get lighted to use IPv6.  I am doing this because some
ads can get through using IPv6 if the IPv4 version is blocked.  Also,
it seems to work fine as far as performance even though it doubles the
file size...

Also added a few comments for better documentation.

Fixes issue #2 whitelist support

Just put a file named whitelist.txt in your home folder.  This file
should contain one domain per line that needs to be whitelisted.  If
the file does not exists, the script will continue as normal.
removed_lines: -cat /tmp/matter.txt | sed $'s/\r$//' | sort | uniq | sed '/^$/d' | awk -v "IP=$piholeIP" '{sub(/\r$/,""); print "address=/"$0"/"IP}' > /tmp/andLight.txt
added_lines: +	cat /tmp/matter.txt | sed $'s/\r$//' | sort | uniq | sed '/^$/d' | grep -v -x -f $whitelist | awk -v "IP=$piholeIP" '{sub(/\r$/,""); print "address=/"$0"/"IP}' > /tmp/andLight.txt
+	cat /tmp/matter.txt | sed $'s/\r$//' | sort | uniq | sed '/^$/d' | awk -v "IP=$piholeIP" '{sub(/\r$/,""); print "address=/"$0"/"IP}' > /tmp/andLight.txt

pi-hole/pi-hole.message:
Removes more duplicates that uniq could not find

http://jacobsalmela.com/raspberry-pi-ad-blocker-advanced-setup/#comment-
1860675175

Thanks to napgravy for figuring this out.  It seems the DOS-style
line-endings that prevented uniq from getting rid of them.  This
reduces the ad domains from ~140,000 to around ~120,000 but it is much
more accurate.
added_lines: +cat /tmp/matter.txt | sed $'s/\r$//' | sort | uniq | sed '/^$/d' | awk -v "IP=$piholeIP" '{sub(/\r$/,""); print "address=/"$0"/"IP}' > /tmp/andLight.txt
